title: Korean War begins
description: North Korea invades South Korea, starting the Korean War.

title: First hydrogen bomb test
description: The United States detonates the first thermonuclear weapon (Ivy Mike) at Enewetak Atoll.

title: Korean War armistice signed
description: An armistice agreement is signed, ending active combat in the Korean War.

title: Rosa Parks refuses to give up her bus seat
description: Rosa Parks is arrested in Montgomery, Alabama, sparking the Montgomery Bus Boycott.

title: Sputnik 1 launched
description: The Soviet Union launches Sputnik 1, the first artificial Earth satellite.

title: Yuri Gagarin becomes first human in space
description: Soviet cosmonaut Yuri Gagarin becomes the first human to journey into outer space.

title: Cuban Missile Crisis begins
description: The United States discovers Soviet ballistic missile deployment in Cuba.

title: Assassination of John F. Kennedy
description: US President John F. Kennedy is assassinated in Dallas, Texas.

title: Civil Rights Act of 1964 signed
description: US President Lyndon Johnson signs the Civil Rights Act, outlawing discrimination.

title: Assassination of Malcolm X
description: Malcolm X is assassinated at the Audubon Ballroom in New York City.

title: Palomares B-52 crash
description: A USAF B-52 collides with a KC-135 tanker over Spain, scattering hydrogen bombs.

title: Six-Day War begins
description: Israel launches pre-emptive strikes against Egypt, Jordan, and Syria.

title: Assassination of Martin Luther King Jr.
description: Civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr. is assassinated in Memphis, Tennessee.

title: Apollo 11 Moon Landing
description: Astronauts Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin become the first humans to walk on the Moon.

title: Watergate break-in
description: Five men are arrested for breaking into the Democratic National Committee headquarters.

title: Paris Peace Accords signed
description: The Paris Peace Accords are signed, ending the United States' direct involvement in the Vietnam War.

title: Richard Nixon resigns
description: Richard Nixon becomes the first US President to resign, due to the Watergate scandal.

title: Fall of Saigon
description: North Vietnamese forces capture Saigon, ending the Vietnam War.

title: United States Bicentennial
description: The United States celebrates its 200th anniversary of independence.

title: Camp David Accords signed
description: Egyptian President Sadat and Israeli PM Begin sign the Camp David Accords.

title: Iran hostage crisis begins
description: Iranian students seize the US Embassy in Tehran, holding 52 Americans hostage.

title: Solidarity trade union formed in Poland
description: Workers at the Lenin Shipyard in Gdańsk go on strike, founding the Solidarity movement.

title: Assassination attempt on Ronald Reagan
description: President Ronald Reagan is shot outside the Washington Hilton Hotel.

title: Korean Air Lines Flight 007 shot down
description: A Soviet jet shoots down a Korean Air Lines Boeing 747, killing all 269 on board.

title: 1984 Summer Olympics open in Los Angeles
description: The Summer Olympic Games open in Los Angeles, boycotted by the Soviet Union.

title: Mikhail Gorbachev becomes Soviet leader
description: Mikhail Gorbachev is named General Secretary of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union.

title: Space Shuttle Challenger disaster
description: Space Shuttle Challenger breaks apart 73 seconds after launch, killing all seven crew members.

title: Chernobyl nuclear disaster
description: Reactor No. 4 at the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant explodes, causing the worst nuclear accident in history.

title: Black Monday stock market crash
description: Global stock markets crash on Black Monday, with the Dow Jones falling 22.6%.

title: Tiananmen Square massacre
description: Chinese troops open fire on pro-democracy protesters in Tiananmen Square.

title: Fall of the Berlin Wall
description: The Berlin Wall falls, marking the beginning of the end of the Cold War.

title: Iraq invades Kuwait
description: Iraqi forces invade and occupy Kuwait, triggering the Gulf War.

title: Gulf War begins (Operation Desert Storm)
description: Coalition forces launch Operation Desert Storm to liberate Kuwait from Iraqi occupation.

title: Dissolution of the Soviet Union
description: Mikhail Gorbachev resigns as president, formally dissolving the Soviet Union.

title: Hurricane Andrew strikes Florida
description: Hurricane Andrew makes landfall in South Florida, causing $27 billion in damage.

title: World Trade Center bombing
description: A truck bomb explodes in the parking garage below the World Trade Center in New York City.

title: Rwandan genocide begins
description: The assassination of Rwandan president Habyarimana triggers a genocide that kills 800,000 Tutsi.

title: Oklahoma City bombing
description: A truck bomb destroys the Alfred P. Murrah Federal Building, killing 168 people.

title: TWA Flight 800 crashes
description: TWA Flight 800 explodes over the Atlantic Ocean off Long Island, New York, killing all 230 aboard.

title: Hong Kong handover
description: The United Kingdom transfers sovereignty over Hong Kong to China.

title: Death of Princess Diana
description: Diana, Princess of Wales, dies in a car crash in Paris.

title: US Embassy bombings in Africa
description: Al-Qaeda bombs the US embassies in Nairobi, Kenya, and Dar es Salaam, Tanzania.

title: NATO bombing of Yugoslavia begins
description: NATO launches an air campaign against the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia.

title: Y2K rollover
description: The world transitions to the year 2000 without major computer failures.

title: September 11 attacks
description: Al-Qaeda hijackers crash planes into the World Trade Center and Pentagon, killing nearly 3,000 people.

title: Invasion of Iraq begins
description: A US-led coalition invades Iraq to remove Saddam Hussein from power.

title: Indian Ocean earthquake and tsunami
description: A 9.1 magnitude earthquake triggers a massive tsunami killing over 230,000 people across 14 countries.

title: Hurricane Katrina strikes New Orleans
description: Hurricane Katrina makes landfall, flooding New Orleans and killing over 1,800 people.

title: First iPhone released
description: Apple releases the first iPhone, transforming the mobile industry.

title: Lehman Brothers bankruptcy
description: Lehman Brothers files for bankruptcy, triggering a global financial crisis.

title: Barack Obama inaugurated as US President
description: Barack Obama is inaugurated as the 44th President of the United States, the first African American to hold the office.

title: Haiti earthquake
description: A 7.0 magnitude earthquake strikes Haiti, killing over 230,000 people.

title: Deepwater Horizon oil spill begins
description: An explosion on the Deepwater Horizon drilling rig triggers the largest marine oil spill in history.

title: Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami
description: A 9.0 magnitude earthquake triggers a tsunami that kills over 15,000 people and causes the Fukushima nuclear disaster.

title: Osama bin Laden killed
description: Al-Qaeda leader Osama bin Laden is killed by US special forces in Abbottabad, Pakistan.

title: Hurricane Sandy strikes the US East Coast
description: Hurricane Sandy makes landfall in New Jersey, causing $65 billion in damage.

title: Boston Marathon bombing
description: Two bombs explode near the finish line of the Boston Marathon, killing 3 and injuring 264.

title: Russia annexes Crimea
description: Russia formally annexes Crimea following a disputed referendum.

title: Paris terrorist attacks
description: Coordinated ISIS attacks across Paris kill 130 people.

title: Brexit referendum
description: The United Kingdom votes to leave the European Union.

title: Donald Trump elected US President
description: Donald Trump defeats Hillary Clinton in the US presidential election.

title: Stoneman Douglas High School shooting
description: A gunman kills 17 students and staff at Marjory Stoneman Douglas High School in Parkland, Florida.

title: Notre-Dame Cathedral fire
description: A fire extensively damages Notre-Dame Cathedral in Paris.

title: United Kingdom leaves the European Union
description: The United Kingdom formally leaves the European Union.

title: COVID-19 declared a global pandemic
description: The World Health Organization declares COVID-19 a pandemic.

title: US Capitol stormed
description: Supporters of President Trump storm the US Capitol building.

title: Russia invades Ukraine
description: Russia launches a full-scale military invasion of Ukraine.
